reference_policy.rationale: Reference-only packages may stay in the core repository only when they remain offline, lightweight, and free of provider/platform SDK dependencies. They can import the Go standard library and this module's own contracts, but production service clients belong in external adapter repositories.

entries.rationale: A2A agent/task/message/artifact/status contracts are SDK integration boundaries. Production gateways and remote discovery policy remain external adapters.
MCP manager/client/server contracts are SDK integration boundaries. OAuth registries and production transport bundles remain external adapters.
Provider registry, router, route set, fake provider, middleware, and error taxonomy are core SDK contracts. Concrete hosted model providers remain external adapters.
A2UI is a protocol-specific channel adapter. Core should keep SurfaceMessage, Transfer, Channel, and schema validator contracts only.
AG-UI is a protocol-specific channel adapter. Core should keep platform-neutral event and channel contracts only.
Lark is a concrete platform adapter with callback signing, user mapping, OAuth, and production delivery concerns outside the SDK core.
HTTP SSE is a small no-account reference channel useful for conformance and examples. Production WebSocket/SSE gateways should live externally.
Writer-based TUI is a local reference implementation for Transfer and Channel contracts, with no external platform dependency.
Google Cloud Storage checkpoint persistence is a production cloud backend and should be versioned outside the core SDK.
Conditional object checkpoint backend is a generic reference for CAS and index repair contracts. Provider-specific object implementations should live externally.
Alibaba Cloud OSS checkpoint persistence is a production cloud backend and should be versioned outside the core SDK.
Cloudflare R2 checkpoint persistence is a production cloud backend and should be versioned outside the core SDK.
Redis checkpoint persistence is a production backend and should be maintained as an external adapter.
AWS S3 checkpoint persistence is a production cloud backend and should be versioned outside the core SDK.
database/sql checkpoint persistence is a production backend. Core should keep the RowBackend and conformance contract.
Channel review is Dev Agent harness behavior. Core should keep ChannelEvent and review evidence contracts only.
CI review is Dev Agent release-gate behavior and should not be maintained in the core SDK repository.
Git diff scanning is Dev Agent evidence collection. Core should keep observed diff evidence contracts only.
Model review is Dev Agent harness behavior. Core should keep reviewer decision evidence contracts only.
HTTP control-plane lease backend is a production integration adapter. Core should keep LeaseBackend and memory reference implementation.
Conditional object lease backend is a generic reference for lease CAS semantics. Provider-specific object adapters should live externally.
Redis lease persistence is a production backend and should be maintained as an external adapter.
database/sql lease persistence is a production backend. Core should keep LeaseBackend and conformance contracts.
OpenAI-compatible model transport is a concrete provider adapter. Core should keep ChatModel, StreamingModel, router, and provider contracts.
Trace exporters and vendor-shaped envelopes are observability adapters. Core should keep events, RunExport, verification, and plugin contracts.
Filesystem blob/object storage is a local reference backend useful for tests and examples, with no external service dependency.
Google Cloud Storage blob/object storage is a production cloud backend and should be versioned outside the core SDK.
Generic object blob backend is a reference for object storage contracts. Provider-specific implementations should live externally.
Alibaba Cloud OSS blob/object storage is a production cloud backend and should be versioned outside the core SDK.
Cloudflare R2 blob/object storage is a production cloud backend and should be versioned outside the core SDK.
AWS S3 blob/object storage is a production cloud backend and should be versioned outside the core SDK.
HTTP control-plane TurnLoop store is a production integration adapter. Core should keep TurnLoopStore contracts and local references.
Conditional object TurnLoop backend is a generic reference for versioned CAS semantics. Provider-specific object adapters should live externally.
Redis TurnLoop persistence is a production backend and should be maintained as an external adapter.
database/sql TurnLoop persistence is a production backend. Core should keep TurnLoop store and conformance contracts.
Agent-as-Tool is a harness template. Core should keep Runnable, Tool, A2A, policy, and event contracts.
Dev Agent is a concrete development harness. Core should keep verification, entropy, policy, review evidence, and resume primitives.
The current ReAct implementation can remain as a minimal reference template and trajectory fixture. Production variants should move to a template repository.
